75608.subject: ‘I’m Now Broke’: These Investors Lost Billions On Trump Stocks And Crypto
75608.body:
Forbes P.M.

Автор: Forbes


Автор: Forbes

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/im-now-broke-investors-lost-billions-trump-stocks-crypto-wprlc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BpEK9qaWrR5uFpWc7X5tPRQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3RNHba4NqEzIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrb5kduq~en-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrb5kduq-en
  
Good evening, In October 2021, Donald Trump unveiled Truth Social, promising a censorship-free platform and offering supporters a chance to invest via a special purpose acquisition company. Shares of the SPAC jumped 1,650% in two days, then fell about 30% by the start of the third. However, the believers didn’t quite see the payday they expected. “Donald Trump is a b----,” Chad Nedohin, a worship leader who once moonlighted as the unofficial captain of Trump Media’s retail shareholders, said on the matter. Read more here. Thanks for reading! Kris Kornbluth Assistant Editor, Platforms & Curation More Recommended Reads Brendan Smialowski/AFP via Getty Images How OpenAI Plans To Win Over Doctors, Patients And Hospitals The AI juggernaut is all-in on the healthcare industry—but it needs to ensure ChatGPT is accurate enough for the millions who already use it for health advice. Illustration by Macy Sinreich for Forbes; Images by fhm via Getty Images, Penpak S via Getty Images AI Data Centers Are Set To Electrify This $13 Billion Family’s 76-Year-Old Company The company has made a bundle for the Richards family, who still owns 100% of the business and is worth approximately $13.1 billion, Forbes estimates. The family fortune will likely continue to grow due to the explosion of AI data centers.
Приєднатися до обговорення
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/im-now-broke-investors-lost-billions-trump-stocks-crypto-wprlc?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BpEK9qaWrR5uFpWc7X5tPRQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3RNHba4NqEzIk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-newsletter_content_preview_text-0-readmore_button_-null-fz96fz~mrb5kduq~en-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrb5kduq-en
  
      

----------------------------------------

Це повідомлення е-поштою адресовано учасникові Oleh Novosadov (I am 36 years old, and I am passionate about the world of cryptocurrencies and blockchain technologies. My professional interests include investing in cryptocurrencies, exploring blockchain, Web3, and the DeFi sector.)

75603.subject: 🔷 The Daily: Polymarket sued over disputed bitcoin bet
75603.body:
SEC plans crypto rule changes for exchanges and broker dealers in 2026 regulatory agenda | Tether's former CIO seeks buyers for part of his stake in the firm | Coinbase secures UK investment services license to add derivatives and equities trading | President Trump's strategic bitcoin reserve hits a snag |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Jason Shubnell

Presented by

BitGo ( https://www.bitgo.com/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Tuesday! With *bitcoin* rebounding ( https://www.theblock.co/post/407414/bitcoins-early-july-bounce-rides-thin-summer-liquidity-as-half-of-supply-still-sits-underwater-analysts?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) above $64,000, analysts say the rally may simply be riding on thin summer liquidity even as onchain signals suggest the market could be nearing a longer-term bottom.

In today's newsletter, traders sue *Polymarket* over *Strategy bitcoin* sale bets, the *SEC* plans crypto rule changes for exchanges and broker dealers, former *Tether* CIO *Richard Heathcote* is seeking buyers for part of his stake, and more.

Meanwhile, institutional crypto platform *EDX Markets* closed a $76 million Series C ( https://www.theblock.co/post/407489/crypto-platform-edx-markets-series-c-funding-sbi-sole-investor?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) funding round, with Japan's *SBI Holdings* as the sole investor.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*🔮 Two traders sue Polymarket over disputed resolution of Strategy bitcoin sale market*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/407368/two-traders-sue-polymarket-strategy-bitcoin-sale?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Two traders, *William Wood* and *Thomas Bush* , sued predictions platform *Polymarket* over its resolution of a market tied to whether *Strategy* would sell *bitcoin*.

* The lawsuit, filed in the New York Supreme Court, names Polymarket, CEO *Shayne Coplan* , CMO *Matthew Modabber* , and other entities and individuals as defendants.
* The plaintiffs held "Yes" shares in a market asking whether Strategy would sell any bitcoin by May 31, after the firm disclosed in an *SEC* filing that it sold 32 BTC between May 26 and 31.
* Polymarket resolved the market as "No" after adding clarifying language that plaintiffs allege effectively re

75599.subject: Beware the top-heavy economy
75599.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzB9AAcAAAAAA7AmLEvrymCmUC24SoZ06Apn2Iqdasd7Vfg4N37jh6Yd49zrhDDsW2-kjEpEXod5pl91jiFg6pbJEon3m2VM6KfNBudQ_UJ87uWYOC_evTZtPg 

July 7th 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzB9AAcAAAAAA7AmLEvsi7rFU66aVMWq0QNO-oZFYgMru0zRcrvDlCB8EFBccJnCyo_nVwwEZS23UUZ_APeuoIhVjk4rcssM8Mnr899HemzX-jiFZ3sL1kTjRg 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzB9AAcAAAAAA7AmLEvtkmNVqy7cKwlDYaUAsHuAKyzsc3G1KTx2Za0moC5iw00Ycj-VQIg-18ZzTGGwoDkLX9ywozhiDzlp40KGTUpuJoJGs8vL2C62kZurWw 


 


 
 

Brooke Unger

Senior digital editor



 The deals keep getting bigger. Everyone knows about SpaceX’s record-breaking initial public offering, but that’s just part of a trend towards supersize capital flows. 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ5MzB9AAcAAAAAA7AmLEvuRnV8sMopULX5K8Ajwuf4v4zvAiDWeKlss819JaMU6WLSKeiwjtnzZcTK5ppmBIcgcl1tuZRoAVp5xFXBM3s1eH6q8DhJ8hQt 
Our analysis  
 explains why that could be a big problem.


 This week The Telegram ponders the curious case of Mark Rutte, NATO’s secretary-general. His tendency to flatter Donald Trump is cringe-making, but he mixes obsequiousness with candour. Our geopolitics columnist explains why Mr Rutte needs to keep serving his

75598.subject: 99 exploits. The most hacked quarter in DeFi history.
75598.body:
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/c2549d42-c592-4e6b-82e8-409bebea29d3/Top_banner.jpg?t=1783431744) [Brought to you by Boardwalk]
Follow image link: (https://defillama.com/research/report/boardwalk-closing-the-gap-between-token-launches-token-economies)
Caption: 



Welcome to the DefiLlama Newsletter. Each week we bring you the most valuable onchain insights from the the deepest DeFi dataset in the world.



Subscribe (https://newsletter.defillama.com)



## Q2 2026: most-hacked quarter on record

Q2 2026 saw more hacks than any quarter on record with 99 hacks in the DefiLlama database.

Q1 2025 holds the “value stolen” record, but 2026 has seen over 140 exploits so far, combining for over $1b in value stolen.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/92aebd94-bd67-494a-9c79-a7de4aa3c5ef/quarterly-crypto-hacks-incident-count-vs-value-stolen_2026-07-07.png?t=1783431632)
Caption: 

April, May and June all saw 30+ exploits, and after BonkDAO’s [malicious governance proposal](https://x.com/bonk_inu/status/2074191403781906800) resulting in a $20m token drain, July isn’t off to a great start either.

Protocol logic exploits were by far the most common attack, but they were smaller on average than infrastructure failures. Drift’s admin key attack and Kelp’s LayerZero bridge exploit represent over 80% of value stolen this quarter.

39 of these hacks happened on Ethereum. The large value stolen totals on Arbitrum and Solana are mostly the result of fewer, larger hacks.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/7b5c127a-f146-4a32-834b-c57852f1f252/q2-2026-crypto-hacks-stolen-value-by-chain-top-10_2026-07-06.png?t=1783357694)
Caption: A single hack can tag multiple chains. Kelp, for example, tagged both Arbitrum and Ethereum. 98% of Arbitrum’s stolen value came from one incident (Kelp); 99.5% of Solana’s came from Drift

Zooming out across all hacks in DefiLlama’s database going back to 2016, private key compromises are responsible for 44% of total value stolen. 16 of Q2’s 99 incidents and 45% of dollar losses trace back to some form of key or access-control compromise.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/54d18331-96bc-4456-be2a-58d2dea6f6b4/total-value-hacked_2026-07-06.png?t=1783364181)
Caption: 

Key management is still one of the biggest preventable risks for protocols. While DeFi users need to remember that “Audited” does not mean “Guaranteed Safe”, enabling multi-party computation, hardware-backed signing and eliminating single-attestor verification are legitimate and available risk mitigators for teams.

## How do hacks impact protocols?

Across hacks with us
